page.primary_keyword: Marbella Feria San Bernabe boat party
page.title: Marbella Feria San Bernabe: Celebrate from a Yacht

data.meta_description: Celebrate Marbella Feria San Bernabe from a yacht. Book a boat party in Puerto Banús with skipper, drinks & snacks included. From €749/2h.
data.summary: Discover how to celebrate the Marbella Feria San Bernabe from a private yacht in Puerto Banús. Compare boat options, prices, and tips for the best on-water feria experience.
key_facts: Feria San Bernabe runs early June in Marbella town, with fairground rides, casetas, and flamenco.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, 10 minutes by water taxi from Marbella’s feria site.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, and VAT.
Tier B Mangusta 80 (white or grey) from €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a jet ski.
Every charter includes licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, snacks, and insurance.
Maximum 12 guests on most yachts; ideal for a small group celebrating the feria.
Book via WhatsApp for pricing on larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86.
Evening charters let you watch the firework display from the water, avoiding crowds.

faq.q: What is the Marbella Feria San Bernabe?
faq.a: It’s Marbella’s annual fair in honour of its patron saint, San Bernabe. Held in early June, it features traditional casetas (marquee bars), live flamenco, fairground rides, and a firework display. The main location is the fairground near the town centre, but the party spills into the port and along the Golden Mile.

faq.q: When does Feria San Bernabe 2025 take place?
faq.a: The feria typically runs for about 10 days, starting the first week of June. Exact dates are confirmed by Marbella town hall each spring. For planning purposes, aim for the first two weekends of June, when the main events and fireworks occur.

faq.q: Can I watch the fireworks from a boat?
faq.a: Yes. Many of our charters anchor near Marbella’s coastline for the evening firework display. Boats like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80 offer excellent elevated views. You’ll avoid the packed promenade and enjoy a private spectacle with drinks and snacks.

faq.q: How do I get from Puerto Banús to the feria site?
faq.a: Puerto Banús is about 6 km south of Marbella town centre. A water taxi from the port to Marbella’s marina takes roughly 10 minutes. Alternatively, a 10-minute taxi ride costs around €15-20. Many guests moor at the port and walk to the feria grounds.

faq.q: What’s included in the boat party price?
faq.a: All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. Spanish IVA (21%) is included in the prices shown. No hidden fees.

faq.q: Which boat is best for a feria party?
faq.a: For a group of up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m) is ideal – it has a spacious flybridge, sundeck, and includes a jet ski. If you’re a smaller group, the Astondoa 40 (9 guests, from €749/2h) is a cost-effective choice. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food or decorations?
faq.a: Yes, you’re welcome to bring additional food, decorations, or a cake. We provide a basic snack platter and drinks, but you can supplement it. Just let us know in advance so we can ensure fridge space. No glassware on board for safety.

faq.q: How do I book a boat for Feria San Bernabe?
faq.a: You can book directly via our website or WhatsApp. For Tier A boats, prices are fixed and bookable online. For larger yachts, we provide a quote within a few hours. Early booking is recommended as June is peak season in Marbella.

data.body_html:
Marbella’s Feria San Bernabe is the town’s biggest annual party — a week of flamenco, fairground rides, and late-night casetas. But the best seat in town isn’t on the fairground: it’s on the water. A Marbella Feria San Bernabe boat party lets you celebrate with your own group, away from the crowds, with the Mediterranean as your backdrop. Here’s how to plan it.

Why celebrate Feria San Bernabe from a yacht?

The feria’s main site is in Marbella town, about 6 km north of Puerto Banús. By boat, you get a front-row view of the evening fireworks over the sea, plus a private space to enjoy drinks, music, and snacks. You can moor near Marbella’s marina and walk into the fair, or stay on the water all evening. No queuing for taxis, no jostling in crowded casetas.

Our fleet for feria boat parties

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús. For a feria party, we recommend these options:

BoatLengthMax guestsPrice rangePerfect for
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h – €2,299/8hSmall groups, budget-friendly
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h – €2,299/8hSmall group with extra space
Mangusta 80 (white/grey)24m12€4,719/4h (includes jet ski)Luxury party with water toys
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12On requestFlybridge views for fireworks
Ferretti 9429m12On requestUltra-luxury, large deck space

For pricing on larger yachts (Canados 86, K80, Maiora 26m), message us on WhatsApp for a custom quote.

What’s included in your feria charter

  • Licensed, English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the full charter duration
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
  • Light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%) — no surprises

You can bring your own food, decorations, or a birthday cake. Just let us know in advance.

How to combine a boat party with the feria

A typical plan: charter from Puerto Banús at 4pm, cruise along the coast to Marbella’s marina (15 minutes). Anchor off the beach near the fairground, enjoy drinks and snacks while the sun sets. At 10pm, watch the fireworks from your deck. Then either return to port or walk into the feria for casetas and dancing. Evening charters from 6pm to midnight work especially well.

Tips for a smooth feria boat experience

  • Book early — June is peak season, and our fleet fills weeks ahead.
  • Dress for evening breezes; the sea can be cooler than the fairground.
  • If you want to visit the feria on foot, moor at Marbella Marina (€30-50 fee, payable locally).
  • For a group of 12, the Mangusta 80 is the best value with the jet ski included.
  • Check the feria schedule for the official fireworks night (usually a Saturday).

Other ways to enjoy Marbella from the water

If you’re in town for the whole feria week, consider a day trip to Cala Honda or a sunset cruise along the Golden Mile. Our Marbella boat rental hub has guides for every occasion.

Frequently asked questions

What is the Marbella Feria San Bernabe?

It’s Marbella’s annual fair in honour of its patron saint, San Bernabe. Held in early June, it features traditional casetas (marquee bars), live flamenco, fairground rides, and a firework display. The main location is the fairground near the town centre, but the party spills into the port and along the Golden Mile.

When does Feria San Bernabe 2025 take place?

The feria typically runs for about 10 days, starting the first week of June. Exact dates are confirmed by Marbella town hall each spring. For planning purposes, aim for the first two weekends of June, when the main events and fireworks occur.

Can I watch the fireworks from a boat?

Yes. Many of our charters anchor near Marbella’s coastline for the evening firework display. Boats like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80 offer excellent elevated views. You’ll avoid the packed promenade and enjoy a private spectacle with drinks and snacks.

How do I get from Puerto Banús to the feria site?

Puerto Banús is about 6 km south of Marbella town centre. A water taxi from the port to Marbella’s marina takes roughly 10 minutes. Alternatively, a 10-minute taxi ride costs around €15-20. Many guests moor at the port and walk to the feria grounds.

What’s included in the boat party price?

All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. Spanish IVA (21%) is included in the prices shown. No hidden fees.

Which boat is best for a feria party?

For a group of up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m) is ideal – it has a spacious flybridge, sundeck, and includes a jet ski. If you’re a smaller group, the Astondoa 40 (9 guests, from €749/2h) is a cost-effective choice. All depart from Puerto Banús.

Can I bring my own food or decorations?

Yes, you’re welcome to bring additional food, decorations, or a cake. We provide a basic snack platter and drinks, but you can supplement it. Just let us know in advance so we can ensure fridge space. No glassware on board for safety.

How do I book a boat for Feria San Bernabe?

You can book directly via our website or WhatsApp. For Tier A boats, prices are fixed and bookable online. For larger yachts, we provide a quote within a few hours. Early booking is recommended as June is peak season in Marbella.

Ready to celebrate Feria San Bernabe in style? Browse our full fleet and book your boat party today. For questions or custom quotes, contact us on WhatsApp — we’ll help you plan the perfect on-water feria experience.
